## [00:00 - 03:03]

Okay, now part five of creating pre-launch content. We're going to just go through some of the other types of pre-launch content pretty quickly here. There are lots of other types of pre-launch content in addition to video. One of them is audio and this is generally not recommended back in the old days. We used to do audio. The thing is if you have a great audio and it might, maybe you did an audio interview with someone that would be great or where you got a few different audio interviews and you're going to put them together into one audio. You can use that, but what you want to do is you want to turn it into screen capture. Just take that audio and then build out some slides for it, build out a mind map for it, get some photos, get some pictures, whatever, and marry up that audio to the screen capture and it will raise the perceived value in a huge way. So generally not a big fan of audio these days. Very rare for people to just come to a website and sit there and click a play button and listen to an audio. Just throw up a screen cap there and turn it into a video and it will really, it will be a lot more impactful. You can do a PDF report. This is where you put together a PDF. Just create a document, turn it into a PDF, which you can do pretty much all kinds of software these days. It's built right into most word processing software like Pages for the Manic or Word. Very easy to create PDFs. You can do it as a written report where you just write sort of like a white paper type of thing. You can do it as a process map, a mind map, a flow chart, an infographic. The great thing about PDFs is they have really high perceived value and people will opt in. If you tell them they can download a PDF. They will opt in for it. Really, really works very, very well. Another thing that works great is mixing a PDF with a video. I do that with my product launch blueprint. I'll have a video, like a lengthy video, but the video is basically me going through the actual PDF. The other thing is it doesn't have to be super long. I've knocked out a great PDF report in a matter of a few hours. If you can do a PDF that's like six to eight pages and then you put in some graphics and a title page and a conclusion, you can end up with a 12, 14, 16 page report pretty darn quickly. PDFs have very high perceived value and can be very, very effective. I do like to match them with videos. If you put those two together, in terms of getting people to opt in to your pre-launch content, that is a winning strategy. There are even more kinds of pre-launch content, such as webinars or tele-seminars. They can be used during your pre-launch or they can be used after you open your cart. I tend to use them more after I open my cart or right at the end of my pre-launch right

## [03:03 - 06:06]

before we are about to open the cart. Of course, a webinar means they can see your screen and you end up with a presentation that's not unlike this. It's not unlike a screen capture. The difference is a webinar, you're delivering that live and people are all dialed in it that one time for the webinar. Tele-seminar is just purely via the telephone. They're just getting you via audio. Lot easier to create a tele-seminar than a webinar because you don't have to bother creating slides and having to worry about what it looks like. Tele-seminar is going to be very easy. Freeconferencecalling.com, it's a place you can get what we call a bridge line. That's how you actually do your tele-seminar. There's a service and you will dial into the service on your phone and you've got a pin code that you type in and that makes you the moderator so you can speak. Other people can dial in and they enter a pin code as an attendee so they can listen. Very, very simple to use, very easy to use. Freeconferencecalling.com, you can actually get a free what we call a bridge line that allows you to do that. If you're going to do a webinar, you can use Instant Tele-seminar or you can use GoToWebinar. Those are two services. GoToWebinar is generally more flexible, a little more powerful and it costs a whole lot more money than Instant Tele-seminar. I've used both of them. They're both very, very nice services. Then you can also, as pre-launch, you can give away software. This is something that I have not done but you can actually create software. Probably not the most super expensive crazy software and you probably just want to use the software as one piece of pre-launch content. Usually either your first piece of pre-launch content or your last piece of pre-launch content. It has really high perceived value. It can be very, very effective. This is not something I've done. I've helped out in a couple launches where they did it but I haven't done this. I'm not much of a software builder at this point in my life. I can't tell you a lot about this from experience but you're on your own with this one. Like I said, I have seen this be very, very effective. If it ties in well with your launch, if you can create some piece of software and you've got experience or comfort doing that, then it's something to consider. So, circling back, remember in terms of creating pre-launch content, it is all about horses for courses. What you use depends on your situation and what you can create and what your assets are. Maybe you just did this amazing interview with some world-class expert in your market that just brings huge authority and social proof to your launch. Now you've got this great audio. Well, turn that into a screen capture video.

## [06:06 - 08:11]

There you go. Boom, you've got it. On the other hand, maybe you're just really great on camera or you're just really well spoken on camera. You've got someone that can shoot video for you. Maybe you're super attractive, whatever. Then use a video. Maybe you're just an incredible writer and you can create incredible reports. Well, use a PDF. So, what you use should be based on your situation and what your assets are and what you're comfortable with. Video is really great for lots of situations and it's good for you to get comfortable with video. Even if you're not comfortable right now, it's a great thing to start working on because, like I said earlier in that first video, in the first video about creating pre-launch in the first of the series, people can connect with you. It's a lot easier to appear human and appear approachable and personable and like you've got a personality than it is for most people to do in writing. It's usually easier to do that on video. People can see you, they can sense your passion, your sincerity. It's great for lots of situations. Even if you're not comfortable with video now, if you just start shooting it now, you'll be comfortable sooner rather than later. It doesn't take that long to get comfortable in front of the camera. And remember, finally, it is about progress. It's not about perfection. You will get better at this. Just go ahead and just knock it out. Get some pre-launch done. If it's not perfect, then you can always do it over again. But it is, it's really about progress and getting better over time. I've improved. I've seen so many of my PLF owners improve over time. It's amazing how you just get better and better by just taking those baby steps and moving forward and increasing your skills a little bit at a time.
